data.title: Coca-Cola, McDonald's, and Philip Morris gain as investors turn defensive amid rising U.S.-China tension
data.description:
U.S. stocks turned lower after President Donald Trump threatened to slap a "massive increase" of tariffs on Chinese products imported into the United States to counter new export controls that China imposed on rare earths from the nation. Notably, Trump also threatened to cancel his upcoming meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping, which adds another level of uncertainty to the trade talks and tariffs.

The tech sector led the downturn, with the Nasdaq 100 down 2.8% at 1:19 p.m. Investors seemed to be looking at the consumer staples sector as a shelter in the sudden trade storm.

The top three gainers in the Dow Jones Industrial Average were Coca-Cola (NYSE:KO), McDonald's (NYSE:MCD), and Walmart (WMT) as investors turned more cautious. In the S&P 500 Index, the two biggest gainers were PepsiCo (PEP) +3.4% and Philip Morris International (NYSE:PM) +2.7%. Consumer defensive names Campbell's Company (CPB), Dollar General (DG), Altria (MO), Mondelez International (MDLZ), and Kroger (KR) were all solidly higher.

The Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLP) was up 0.5% to outperform major market indexes and broad retail ETFs. XLP has been an underperformer in 2025 amid the rush into AI stocks and crypto names. The top ten holdings in the fund are Walmart (WMT), Costco (COST), Procter & Gamble (PG), Coca-Cola (NYSE:KO), Philip Morris International (NYSE:PM), Mondelez International (MDLZ), Altria Group (MO), PepsiCo (PEP), Colgate-Palmolive (CL), and Monster Beverage (MNST).

data.title: BLS will publish September CPI data by month-end
data.description:
The Department of Labor's Bureau of Labor Statistics said on Friday that it will publish the September 2025 Consumer Price Index on Oct. 24, 2025, which will provide the Federal Reserve with important inflation data before its next meeting on Oct. 28-29.

However, the agency won't resume the release of any other economic data until the government re-opens, it said in a statement. The New York Times reported that the CPI report might be released before the Fed meeting.

The CPI data is needed for the Social Security Administration to meet deadlines "to ensure the accurate and timely payment of benefits," the BLS said.

data.title: Align Tech cut to Hold at Jefferies after clear aligner survey
data.description:
Shares of Align Technology (NASDAQ:ALGN) fell ~5% on Friday after Jefferies downgraded the dental product manufacturer to Hold from Buy, citing results from a U.S. dentist survey that indicated multiple headwinds for the clear aligner maker.

Analyst Michael Sarcone pointed out that the survey conducted in early September involving 49 orthodontists and general dentists suggested that until 2030, there will be “only modest, and underwhelming, incremental clear aligner penetration.”

Additionally, the survey respondents have, on average, expected Align Tech’s (NASDAQ:ALGN) market share to drop to 70% in 2026 from 77% in 2024, with rival AngelAlign being the leading share taker. According to Jefferies, 22% of doctors have also indicated potential demand declines in the near term due to macro-related headwinds.

While Align (NASDAQ:ALGN) tech, trading at a historically low valuation, “seems relatively attractive,” the analyst expects its multiple expansion to come under pressure due to a lack of near-term catalysts and macro headwinds.  Sarcone slashed his price target on ALGN to $140 from $215 per share.

data.title: SA Asks: What's the best gold play for investors right now?
data.description:
Gold, the perennial safe haven, is once again in the spotlight, with prices hitting an all-time high of over $4,000 per ounce earlier this week.

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Samuel Smith and Taylor Dart what they thought was the best gold play right now for investors.

Samuel Smith: To maximize gains from continued upside in the gold price, miner plays like VanEck Gold Miners ETF (NYSEARCA:GDX) and VanEck Junior Gold Miners ETF (NYSEARCA:GDXJ) are compelling choices given that an increase in gold prices has an outsized impact on their profits. 

While B2Gold (NYSE:BTG) has more than doubled since we bought it earlier this year at High Yield Investor, it remains a compelling gold mining investment opportunity, given that it is undervalued relative to many of its peers and has numerous growth catalysts (Goose ramp-up, Fekola expansion project, and attractive long-term production potential from Gramalote).

Once prepaid gold deliveries are completed by the middle of next year, Goose fully ramps up, and capital expenditures begin to subside in 2026, BTG will likely see significant free cash flow growth, assuming gold prices remain elevated. This could lead to an increase in buybacks and/or dividends, potentially catalyzing shares materially higher in the process.

Taylor Dart: Over the past year, several gold producers of varying quality have exploded off their lows given the ascent we've seen in gold prices that has resulted in exponential margin expansion. However, with money often flowing into the producers first and the developers second, there's a small handful of developers still on the sale rack. And while not all developers are created equal, as many own weaker assets and/or operate in less attractive jurisdictions, Liberty Gold (OTCQX:LGDTF) is one name that stands out as quality at a very attractive price.

The company's flagship asset is its Black Pine Project in Idaho, home to a 4.2+ million ounce and growing gold resource with attractive grades for a run-of-mine oxide heap leach operation. Following capital raises that have seen participation from Centerra Gold (CGAU) and Wheaton Precious Metals (WPM), and despite the stock's recent rally, Liberty Gold trades at a fully diluted market cap of just ~$300 million, or just 0.35x its estimated net asset value of $830 million using a conservative $3,100/oz gold price and 8% discount rate. 

At a $3,500/oz gold price, Black Pine's NAV increases to an estimated $1.14 billion, leaving Liberty Gold trading at just 0.26x NAV despite owning a Tier-1 jurisdiction project capable of producing ~183,000 ounces per year (first 5 years) at sub $1,300/oz costs with a considerable mine life (~20 years).

Importantly, this P/NAV multiple values its Goldstrike Project (gold + antimony) in Utah at zero for conservatism, with investors expected to get a spin-out of this asset over the next twelve months. So, with Liberty Gold offering nearly 200% upside to its fair value estimate of US$1.20/share and trading at barely 1x FY2030 EV/FCF estimates ($3,500/oz gold price assumption) or just 0.90x 2030 EV/FCF estimates at a $3,800/oz gold price assumption, I continue to see Liberty Gold (OTCQX:LGDTF) as a steal at current levels for patient investors looking for gold exposure without paying a lofty price, with the bonus of a spin-out in its #2 asset, Goldstrike.

  • Top Gold ETFs
  • Top Gold Stocks

data.title: Wells Fargo’s top 6 retail stocks amid slowing consumer trends
data.description:
Wells Fargo’s retailing, specialty softlines and e-commerce analytics team identified six promising stock investments despite expectations of a slower start to the fourth quarter and increasingly cautious outlook heading into the holiday season.

“With the 2H setup getting increasingly more complex, we focus on a handful of names with the most compelling idio-stories in the space,” said Ike Boruchow, senior equity consumer analyst at Wells Fargo Securities.

Kontoor Brands (KTB) emerges as the analyst’s top pick heading into 2026, positioned to weather the challenging retail environment. The recommendation comes as Wells Fargo’s traffic tracking shows September marking “one of the slowest foot traffic months of 2025,” with credit card data likewise suggesting sluggish spending to end the month, likely resulting from a combination of “weather, macro and pull-forward” factors, including the “back-to-school” sales.

Among off-price retailers, Ross Stores (ROST) stands out as the best investment play according to the research, while Tapestry (TPR) is highlighted for “riding Coach momentum.”

Boruchow noted that the Coach brand “has not run a promo for many weeks now, reflecting the strength of the brand,” with both full-price and outlet channels remaining “very clean” with “essentially no material promos” since August.

Bath & Body Works (BBWI) earned a spot on the list due to its attractive “valuation + CEO change catalyst,” while Capri Holdings Limited (CPRI) was characterized as a “misunderstood” idiosyncratic name with potential despite seeing significant downward EPS forecast revisions of 44% over the past year.

Wells Fargo’s sentiment index shows Tapestry (TPR) consistently remaining among the top four stocks year-to-date “given their strong overall performance and fundamentals.”

The RealReal (REAL) rounds out the recommendations as “the top pick among e-comm names with category momentum and AI improvements driving scale,” according to Boruchow.

Despite ongoing concerns about tariffs and macroeconomic pressures, the retail sector has demonstrated “remarkable resiliency” with positive trends year-to-date, though the analyst warned that inventory is built and “absent an [acceleration] in spend, we could be bracing for a promotional holiday season ahead.”

Here are more details of the picks:

  • Kontoor Brands (KTB) – 2025 P/E: 14.2x; Price target: $100
  • Ross Stores (ROST) – 2025 P/E: 24.1x; Price target: $175
  • Tapestry (TPR) – 2025 P/E: 22.2x; Price target: $120
  • Bath & Body Works (BBWI) – 2025 P/E: 7.4x; Price target: $44
  • Capri Holdings Limited (CPRI) – 2025 P/E: 16.7x; Price target: $25
  • RealReal (REAL) – 2025 P/E: 46.0x; Price target: $15

data.title: Tech stocks tumble after Trump mulls 'massive increase' in tariffs on Chinese imports
data.description:
Tech stocks tumbled on Friday after U.S. President Donald Trump said he was thinking of a "massive increase" in tariffs on Chinese products after China tightened its rare earth export rules on Thursday.

"They are becoming very hostile, and sending letters to Countries throughout the World, that they want to impose Export Controls on each and every element of production having to do with Rare Earths, and virtually anything else they can think of, even if it’s not manufactured in China," said Trump in a post on Truth Social.

On Thursday, China tightened its rare earth export rules that could create issues for an array of companies involved in the semiconductor supply chain, as these elements are crucial in the manufacturing of many high-tech products.

"For every Element that they have been able to monopolize, we have two. I never thought it would come to this but perhaps, as with all things, the time has come. Ultimately, though potentially painful, it will be a very good thing, in the end, for the U.S.A. One of the Policies that we are calculating at this moment is a massive increase of Tariffs on Chinese products coming into the United States of America. There are many other countermeasures that are, likewise, under serious consideration," said Trump.

Trump and his Chinese counterpart, Xi Jinping are slated to meet in the coming weeks. However, following China's move, the meeting may not happen, according to Trump. "I was to meet President Xi in two weeks, at APEC, in South Korea, but now there seems to be no reason to do so."

"Nobody has ever seen anything like this but, essentially, it would “clog” the Markets, and make life difficult for virtually every Country in the World, especially for China. We have been contacted by other Countries who are extremely angry at this great Trade hostility, which came out of nowhere," Trump went on to say in his post.

AI chipmakers Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) fell about 2%, while Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) tumbled around 6%. Broadcom (AVGO) slipped nearly 3%, while Qualcomm (QCOM) fell roughly 5%. Earlier today it was reported that a Chinese regulator started an investigation into the months-old Qualcomm's acquisition of Autotalks.

Shares of GlobalFoundries (GFS) slumped nearly 6%, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) and Lattice Semiconductor (LSCC) fell about 4% each. Analog Devices (ADI) declined around 3%, Marvell Technology (MRVL) and Texas Instruments (TXN) fell about 2% each. Intel (INTC) dipped nearly 1%.

Meanwhile, chip equipment makers: Lam Research (LRCX) tumbled about 5%, ASML (ASML) and KLA (KLAC) nearly 4%, Applied Materials (AMAT) around 3%.

Several software stocks were also in the red on Friday. Shares of software giant Microsoft (MSFT) dipped about 1%, while creative software maker Adobe (ADBE) declined nearly 2%. Palantir Technologies (PLTR), which develops software platforms for the intelligence community, also fell around 2%.

Asana (ASAN) tumbled about 7%, while Pegasystems (PEGA) declined nearly 3%. Innodata (INOD), Snowflake (SNOW), and monday.com (MNDY) each dipped around 2%. Salesforce (CRM), Workday (WDAY), and Atlassian (TEAM) fell about 1% each. Oracle (ORCL) seemed to buck the trend and was in green.

Several cybersecurity stocks were also among the losers. Fortinet (FTNT) slumped about 3%. CrowdStrike (CRWD) and  Datadog (DDOG) declined around 2% each, while Cloudflare (NET) dipped nearly 1%.

data.title: Novo Nordisk said to be closing its cell therapy unit
data.description:
Ozempic maker Novo Nordisk (NVO) is closing its cell therapy division as part of a major restructuring drive initiated under the company’s new CEO Mike Doustdar, Bloomberg News reported on Thursday, citing the Danish newspaper Borsen.

According to Borsen, all 250 staff members in the division have been notified that they will be facing job cuts. The Danish drugmaker didn’t disclose details but added that it was seeking partners to develop the innovations, Bloomberg News reported. 

The layoffs are expected to impact studies for an experimental heart-failure stem cell therapy and a Parkinson’s disease therapy developed by the company and its partners.Novo (NVO) has reportedly notified the Japanese biotech Heartseed Inc. this week that it intends to terminate a licensing deal for the stem cell therapy.

Jacob Sten Petersen, Novo’s (NVO) global research head, notified the employees of the cell therapy unit about the job cuts. “I would like to once again acknowledge the talented individuals we have had to let go,” Borsen quoted Petersen as saying in an emailed statement. “Your efforts are deeply appreciated.”

The layoffs are the latest to impact Novo Nordisk (NVO) after the company announced plans to eliminate about 9,000 roles globally in a bid to deliver nearly $1.25B in annualized cost savings by the end of 2026 under Doustdar, who assumed leadership in August.

data.title: OpenAI to invest $25B in Argentina with new Stargate project
data.description:
• OpenAI (OPENAI) is going to invest $25B into Argentina as part of a new Stargate project, and bring its generative artificial intelligence services to South America.
  • The maker of ChatGPT and U.S.-based Sur Energy signed a letter of intent for a new data center project in the country, the Argentinian government said on Friday. 
  • “This milestone goes beyond infrastructure,” Altman was quoted as saying in Argentinian news outlet La Derecha Diario. “It's about putting artificial intelligence in the hands of people throughout Argentina.” 
  • The data center will have a capacity of 500 megawatts, the news outlet added, noting it will be the largest in Latin America.
  • OpenAI did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: HAS, PEP, Z, PATH
data.description:
Recent analyst actions have focused on upgrading companies like Hasbro (NASDAQ:HAS) and PepsiCo (NASDAQ:PEP). These upgrades are driven by robust growth in select segments, offering positive outlooks for the companies’ futures. In contrast, downgrades for UiPath (NYSE:PATH) and Zillow (NASDAQ:Z) reveal concerns over demand stability and regulatory hurdles. The latest evaluations underscore the varying challenges and opportunities present across different market sectors.

Upgrades

  • Hasbro Inc. (NASDAQ:HAS): Upgrade Hold to Buy by Seeking Profits. The analyst notes strength from the company’s Wizards and Digital segments, which are offsetting weaknesses in its consumer products division.

    “With buybacks coming into the horizon over the next 12 months and HAS guiding conservatively, shares look increasingly attractive…Wizards momentum shows no sign of stopping.”

  • PepsiCo Inc. (NASDAQ:PEP): Upgrade Hold to Buy by Luca Socci. Despite recent challenges, insights from Elliott Investment Management indicate a potential turnaround through strategic restructuring.

    “The biggest catalyst for Pepsi is the potential turnaround. If Pepsi sells its bottling business and manages to raise margins, the stock will react positively...All in all, I am upgrading Pepsi to a buy. I am not convinced by its fundamentals and by the macroeconomic data. However, I think that when Elliott moves several billion, the likelihood of a positive outcome over a 12-24 month period increases.”

Downgrades

  • UiPath Inc. (NYSE:PATH): Downgrade Buy to Hold by Bay Area Ideas. Although the company is making progress in Agentic Automation, slowing ARR growth and retention rate issues pose concerns.

    “UiPath has all the ingredients to be an Agentic leader… However, perhaps adoption and demand haven’t been great so far. ARR growth slowdown and retention rate deterioration are potentially ominous signals.”

  • Zillow Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:Z): Downgrade Buy to Neutral by Gary Alexander. The uncertainty looming from the FTC’s lawsuit against Zillow’s Rentals segment presents a significant risk.

    “While I typically think lawsuits are a mere distraction for most companies, I am particularly concerned about fallout impacts that the FTC suit could have on Zillow's Rentals business, which has become the lifeblood of the company's growth. Considering the substantial added uncertainty on this stock, I'm downgrading my position on Zillow to "Neutral.”

data.title: U.S. stocks sink as renewed tariff concerns ignite volatility amid continued government shutdown
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages opened in the green, but reversed course on Friday as U.S. President Donald Trump said China has "become very hostile" sending letters to other countries wanting to "impose export controls."

This comes as the U.S. entered its tenth day of government shutdown, while a consumer sentiment survey came in unchanged, but better than expected.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -1.5% in afternoon trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was last -2%, and the Dow (DJI) was -1.2%.

On a Truth Social post, Trump said, "We have been contacted by other Countries who are extremely angry at this great Trade hostility, which came out of nowhere. Our relationship with China over the past six months has been a very good one, thereby making this move on Trade an even more surprising one.”

“The China trade war is getting hot. The Chinese seem unhappy about peace in the Middle East as they’ve been using it to divide our nation on their TikTok platform. Last night they began to threaten rare earth exports crucial to the US. Trump is now looking at a tougher tariff stance,” said Ross Gerber, co-Founder, president and CEO of Gerber Kawasaki Wealth and Investment Management.

The University of Michigan's U.S. consumer sentiment survey showed sentiment barely slowing to 55.0, down from 55.1 in September, and better than the 54.1 expected. Also, the 1-year inflation expectation came in at 4.6%, vs. 4.7% consensus.

In addition, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics has reportedly recalled some workers to get the September retail inflation report, even as the U.S. government shutdown continues.

Over in the bond market, yields were lower. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 7 basis points lower at 4.07%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 7 basis points to 3.54%.

“Fed Governor Waller is on the wires stating emphatically that the U.S. labor market is the furthest thing from being tight, wage growth is cooling which means that the supply-shortage narrative is overblown, and that all this leads to no second-round inflation impact from the tariff hikes. Just in case you’re wondering why the Treasury market is rallying,” said David Rosenberg, founder and president of Rosenberg Research & Associates.

Also within commodities, gold (XAUUSD:CUR) retreated from the $4,000 level, while silver (XAGUSD:CUR) has surpassed the $50 per ounce level. 

Wall Street's major indexes struggled to keep up momentum on Thursday and ended lower, as the U.S. Senate failed to pass two funding bills to end the government shutdown. Meanwhile, the Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell did not comment on the economic outlook or monetary policy at a Fed event.
